Description: Backup validation and disaster recovery checks

Checks.PassMessage: System state backups are current on all DCs
Checks.FailMessage: System state backup age issues detected

EvaluationRules.Message: System state backup age exceeds safe restore threshold
System state backup age approaching tombstone limit
No system state backup found on domain controller
Backup age issue detected on domain controller

Checks.Help: Verifies that a recent system state backup exists for each DC. Without recent backups, recovery from AD corruption or accidental deletion may be impossible. Microsoft recommends daily backups.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Open Windows Server Backup on each DC. 2. Schedule a daily system state backup to a local or network location. 3. Verify backups complete successfully in the event log (Event ID 4). 4. Alternatively use Windows Server Backup CLI: wbadmin start systemstatebackup.

Checks.PassMessage: AD Recycle Bin is enabled - deleted objects can be recovered
Checks.FailMessage: AD Recycle Bin is not enabled

EvaluationRules.Message: AD Recycle Bin not enabled - deleted objects cannot be easily recovered

Checks.Help: Checks whether the Active Directory Recycle Bin optional feature is enabled. When enabled, deleted objects can be restored with all attributes intact without authoritative restore.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Verify Forest Functional Level is Windows Server 2008 R2 or higher. 2. Run: Enable-ADOptionalFeature -Identity 'Recycle Bin Feature' -Scope ForestOrConfigurationSet -Target (Get-ADForest).Name. 3. Note: this change is irreversible - ensure all DCs are upgraded first.

Checks.PassMessage: All VSS writers are stable on all DCs
Checks.FailMessage: VSS writer failures detected - backups may fail

EvaluationRules.Message: Multiple VSS writers failed - AD backups will likely fail
One or more VSS writers not in stable state

Checks.Help: Checks that all Volume Shadow Copy Service (VSS) writers are in a healthy state. Failed VSS writers prevent successful backups of AD, SQL, Exchange and other VSS-aware applications.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Run 'vssadmin list writers' to identify failed writers. 2. Note the writer name and associated service. 3. Restart the associated Windows service (e.g. 'net stop wbengine && net start wbengine'). 4. If persistent, reboot the DC during a maintenance window.

Checks.FailMessage: Tombstone lifetime is below recommended minimum

EvaluationRules.Message: Tombstone lifetime is critically low - DC restoration from backup may fail
Tombstone lifetime is below the Microsoft-recommended 180 days

Checks.Help: Checks the tombstone lifetime (how long deleted objects persist before permanent removal). Values below 180 days reduce the recovery window; below 60 days may prevent successful backup restoration.
Checks.Remediation: 1. Open ADSIEdit and connect to the Configuration partition. 2. Navigate to CN=Directory Service,CN=Windows NT,CN=Services,CN=Configuration,DC=... 3. Set the tombstoneLifetime attribute to 180 (days). 4. Changes take effect without restart.
